---
title: "Teaching"
listing:
  - id: ay_25-26
    contents: ay_25-26.yml
    sort: "date desc"
    template: ../html/teaching/listing.ejs
  - id: ay_24-25
    contents: ay_24-25.yml
    sort: "date desc"
    template: ../html/teaching/listing.ejs
  - id: ay_23-24
    contents: ay_23-24.yml
    sort: "date desc"
    template: ../html/teaching/listing.ejs
  - id: ay_22-23
    contents: ay_22-23.yml
    sort: "date desc"
    template: ../html/teaching/listing.ejs
  - id: ay_21-22
    contents: ay_21-22.yml
    sort: "date desc"
    template: ../html/teaching/listing.ejs
  - id: ay_20-21
    contents: ay_20-21.yml
    sort: "date desc"
    template: ../html/teaching/listing.ejs
  - id: ay_19-20
    contents: ay_19-20.yml
    sort: "date desc"
    template: ../html/teaching/listing.ejs
  - id: ay_17-19
    contents: 
      - ay_18-19.yml
      - ay_17-18.yml
    sort: "date desc"
    template: ../html/teaching/listing.ejs
  - id: ay_older
    contents: ay_older.yml
    sort: "date desc"
    template: ../html/teaching/listing.ejs
title-block-banner: "#EE6A24"
page-layout: full
toc: true
toc-location: right
toc-title: Contents
toc-expand: 3
format:
  html:
    include-in-header: 
      - text: |
          <style>
          .card-description {
            font-size: 0.85em;
            font-weight: 300;
            color: #343a40;
          }
          </style>
---

## Interactive resources

With the power of OJS and Quarto, I've created a few interactive websites to illustrate trickier statistical concepts when teaching. Check them out (and adapt and copy as much as you want!):

:::::: {.grid}

::::: {.card .g-col-12 .g-col-sm-4}

[![](img/hack-your-way.svg){.card-img-top}](https://stats.andrewheiss.com/hack-your-way/)

:::: {.card-body}
Play with different variables until you get the p-value you want! 

[{{< fa arrow-up-right-from-square >}} Site](https://stats.andrewheiss.com/hack-your-way/){.card-link} [{{< fa brands github >}} Source](https://github.com/andrewheiss/hack-your-way){.card-link}

::: {.card-description}
Recreation of FiveThirtyEight's now-disappeared original "Hack Your Way To Scientific Glory"
:::
::::

:::::

::::: {.card .g-col-12 .g-col-sm-4}

[![](img/null-worlds.png){.card-img-top}](https://nullworlds.andrewheiss.com/)

:::: {.card-body}
Learn the intuition behind p-values through simulation

[{{< fa arrow-up-right-from-square >}} Site](https://nullworlds.andrewheiss.com/){.card-link} [{{< fa brands github >}} Source](https://github.com/andrewheiss/null-world-testing){.card-link}

::: {.card-description}
Based on [{infer}](https://infer.netlify.app/) and Allen Downey's philosophy that [there is only one statistical test](https://allendowney.blogspot.com/2016/06/there-is-still-only-one-test.html)
:::

::::

:::::

::::: {.card .g-col-12 .g-col-sm-4}

[![](img/almanac.svg){.card-img-top}](https://dags.andrewheiss.com/)

:::: {.card-body}
A practical field guide to causal graphs, *do*‑calculus, and identification

[{{< fa arrow-up-right-from-square >}} Site](https://dags.andrewheiss.com/){.card-link} [{{< fa brands github >}} Source](https://github.com/andrewheiss/interactive-dags){.card-link}
::::

:::::

::::::

## Courses

### 2025–26

:::{#ay_25-26}
:::

### 2024–25

:::{#ay_24-25}
:::

### 2023–24

:::{#ay_23-24}
:::

### 2022–23

:::{#ay_22-23}
:::

### 2021–22

:::{#ay_21-22}
:::

### 2020–21

:::{#ay_20-21}
:::

### 2019–20

:::{#ay_19-20}
:::

### 2017–19

:::{#ay_17-19}
:::

### 2007–15

:::{#ay_older}
:::
